Corrections.PreviousClaim: Summary states 'All 12 findings are retained with verified line-level source evidence'
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: All 12 findings are verified accurate against source, but the previous answer only covered 10 of 20 true vulnerabilities — missing 10 critical/high vulnerabilities (SC-V3-002, SC-V3-003, SC-V3-009, SC-V3-011, SC-V3-012, SC-V3-014, SC-V3-015, SC-V3-016, SC-V3-017, SC-V3-018).
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Items.Key: buffer overflow in evaluate_expression - incorrect length comparison|buffer overflow|cwe-120|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|830|833|evaluate_expression|line 831: 'if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_))' uses >= instead of <=. line 832: 'strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());' copies the full expression into a 1024-byte stack buffer (defined at line 664 as stack_buffer_size=1024) even when the expression exceeds the buffer size.|any expression longer than 1024 characters causes a stack buffer overflow, enabling arbitrary code execution via shellcode injection into the stack frame.|user input exceeding 1024 characters passed to evaluate_expression|change line 831 to 'if (expression.length() > sizeof(input_buffer_))' and truncate the expression before copying, or remove strcpy entirely and use safe_string_copy or std::string.->buffer overflow in evaluate_expression - incorrect length comparison|buffer overflow|cwe-120|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|830|833|evaluate_expression|line 831: 'if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_))' uses >= instead of >. line 832: 'strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());' copies the full expression into a 1024-byte stack buffer (defined at line 664 as stack_buffer_size=1024) even when the expression exceeds the buffer size, causing an off-by-one overflow on the null terminator.|any expression longer than 1024 characters causes a stack buffer overflow, enabling arbitrary code execution via shellcode injection into the stack frame.|user input exceeding 1024 characters passed to evaluate_expression|change line 831 to 'if (expression.length() > sizeof(input_buffer_))' and truncate the expression before copying, or remove strcpy entirely and use safe_string_copy or std::string.

command injection in execute_admin_command|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|620|execute_admin_command|line 607-608: 'std::string cmd = command.substr(5);' extracts user input after 'exec ' prefix. line 608: 'system(cmd.c_str());' passes the unvalidated user-controlled command directly to system(). the command is only checked for the 'exec' prefix at line 605.|an attacker who gains admin access can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to full system compromise, data theft, or remote code execution.|authenticated admin user (or bypassed authentication) typing 'exec <any command>' in the admin interface|use execve() with explicit argument arrays instead of system(), or validate that cmd contains only allowed characters and uses a whitelist of permitted commands.->command injection in execute_admin_command|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|620|execute_admin_command|line 606-607: 'std::string cmd = command.substr(5);' extracts user input after 'exec ' prefix. line 608: 'system(cmd.c_str());' passes the unvalidated user-controlled command directly to system(). the command is only checked for the 'exec' prefix at line 605.|an attacker who gains admin access can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to full system compromise, data theft, or remote code execution.|authenticated admin user (or bypassed authentication) typing 'exec <any command>' in the admin interface|use execve() with explicit argument arrays instead of system(), or validate that cmd contains only allowed characters and uses a whitelist of permitted commands.

command injection in append_override_audit_event|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|append_override_audit_event|line 626: 'std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path;' constructs a shell command with unvalidated temp directory path. line 630: 'std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\"";' same issue on linux. line 632: 'system(command.c_str());' executes the constructed command.|an attacker who can control the temp/tmpdir environment variable can inject arbitrary commands into the shell, achieving remote code execution.|successful emergency_override authentication (after 5 failed attempts, line 592-598), which triggers this function|use file* with fopen/fwrite/fclose instead of system() to write audit logs, or use std::ofstream for safe file writing.->command injection in append_override_audit_event|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|append_override_audit_event|line 626: 'std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path;' constructs a shell command with unvalidated temp directory path. line 630: 'std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\"";' same issue on linux. line 632: 'system(command.c_str());' executes the constructed command.|an attacker who can control the temp/tmpdir environment variable can inject arbitrary commands into the shell, achieving remote code execution.|successful emergency_override authentication (after 5 failed attempts, line 592-598), which triggers this function|use file* with fopen/fwrite/fclose instead of system() to write audit logs, or use std::ofstream for safe file writing.

truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 previous answer against 20 true vulnerabilities in enhanced_calc.cpp. Previous answer correctly identified 10 of 20 vulnerabilities (50% recall). 10 critical/high vulnerabilities were missed: integer overflow in factorial, use-after-free in memory pool, heap overflow in expression parser, weak PRNG, thread-unsafe timestamp cache, persistent auth state, unhandled exception in variable parsing, integer underflow in block split, unbounded parser recursion, and data race in result cache. All 12 previous findings are evidence-accurate with no false positives detected.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Finding 2: 'Format String Vulnerability in log_debug_message', CWE-134, severity High, confidence 0.95. Evidence: 'Line 238: printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input); passes user-controlled format string (active_format, settable via set_runtime_log_format at line 213-215 or loaded from config at line 556) directly to printf.'
Items.Notes: Source line 238 is exactly printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input); and active_format is settable via set_runtime_log_format() and loaded from config. Previous answer correctly identified this.

Previous answer did not identify the integer overflow in the factorial function (math_engine::FunctionRegistry constructor, lines 312-320). The fact() lambda uses long long result = 1; with result *= i in a loop with no overflow check. This is a clear CWE-190 vulnerability that was not found.

Previous answer did not identify the use-after-free in stage_deferred_history_flush. The function allocates scratch via memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(), passes it to thread_pool_->enqueue() which runs outside the pool mutex, then memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup() is called from SuperCalc::cleanup() which frees all blocks including scratch pointers still in flight. This is a CWE-416.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Finding 6: 'Command Injection in validate_config', CWE-78, severity Critical, confidence 0.95. Evidence: 'Line 564: std::string validator_cmd = get_setting("validator_path", "/usr/bin/validator"); and Line 565: std::string config_file = get_setting("config_file", "default.conf"); retrieve user-controlled values from config file. Line 566: std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file; concatenates them. Line 567: int result = system(command.c_str()); executes the concatenated command.'
Items.Notes: Source lines 564-567 exactly match the previous answer's evidence. The vulnerability is correctly identified as command injection via config validation.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Finding 1: 'Buffer Overflow in safe_string_copy - Off-by-one write past destination buffer', CWE-120, severity High, confidence 0.95. Evidence: 'Line 203: if (src_len > dest_size) src_len = dest_size; caps src_len to dest_size. Line 204: memcpy(dest, src, src_len); copies dest_size bytes to dest[0..dest_size-1]. Line 205: dest[src_len] = '\0'; writes at index dest_size, which is one past the end of the dest buffer.'
Items.Notes: Source lines 203-205 exactly match. The off-by-one overflow at dest[dest_size] is correctly identified.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Finding 12: 'Non-Atomic Volatile Counter in ThreadSafeCounter', CWE-362, severity Medium, confidence 0.7. Evidence: 'Line 478: volatile long long value_; uses volatile instead of std::atomic for thread safety. Line 483-485: long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1; - while a mutex is used, the volatile keyword does not provide atomic semantics.'
Items.Notes: Source line 478 is exactly volatile long long value_; and the design inconsistency is correctly noted.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Finding 8: 'Emergency Authentication Bypass', CWE-287, severity High, confidence 0.95. Evidence: 'Lines 592-598: After login_attempts_ exceeds 5, the string EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE in the input field grants authenticated access without requiring the correct password: if (input.find("EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE") != std::string::npos) { authenticated_ = true; session_token_ = "EMERGENCY_SESSION"; ... return true; }'
Items.Notes: Source lines 592-598 exactly match. The emergency override bypassing authentication is correctly identified.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Finding 9: 'Path Traversal in ConfigLoader', CWE-22, severity Medium, confidence 0.85. Evidence: 'Line 543: std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename; concatenates user-controlled filename directly to the config directory without validating for path traversal characters (e.g., ../).' Line 546: std::ifstream file(full_path); opens the resulting path, which could be outside the intended directory.'
Items.Notes: Source line 543 is exactly std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename; and the path traversal risk is correctly identified.

Previous answer did not identify the heap overflow in ExpressionParser. Source lines 364-367 show buffer_size_ = 256 and expression_buffer_ = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(buffer_size_)). Then line 376: if (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str()); copies arbitrary-length data into a 256-byte heap buffer. This is CWE-122.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Finding 7: 'Hardcoded Admin Credentials in Source Code', CWE-798, severity High, confidence 0.95. Evidence: 'Line 73: constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH"; stores the admin password as a compile-time constant. Line 587: if (input == config::security::ADMIN_SECRET) directly compares user input against this hardcoded value.'
Items.Notes: Source line 73 is exactly constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH"; and line 587 compares against it. Correctly identified.

Previous answer did not identify the weak PRNG. Source lines 636-640 show: std::random_device rd; std::mt19937 gen(rd()); std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999); return "TOKEN_" + std::to_string(dis(gen));. std::random_device is not guaranteed cryptographically secure, and 4-digit tokens are trivially guessable. This is CWE-338.

Previous answer did not identify the thread-unsafe timestamp cache. Source lines 230-231 show: static std::tm cached_time; if (auto* local_time = localtime(&time_t)) cached_time = *local_time;. The static cached_time is accessed without synchronization in a function that may be called from multiple threads. This is CWE-362.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Finding 5: 'Command Injection in append_override_audit_event', CWE-78, severity Critical, confidence 0.95. Evidence: 'Line 626: std::string command = "cmd /C echo Admin override logged >> " + audit_path; constructs a shell command with unvalidated temp directory path. Line 630: std::string command = "sh -c \"echo Admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\""; same issue on Linux. Line 632: system(command.c_str()); executes the constructed command.' Impact: 'An attacker who can control the TEMP/TMPDIR environment variable can inject arbitrary commands into the shell.'
Items.Notes: Source lines 624-633 exactly match. The TEMP/TMPDIR environment variable injection is correctly identified as insecure temporary file handling.

Previous answer did not identify persistent authentication state. Source lines 579-583 show: bool authenticated_; std::string session_token_; static std::atomic<int> login_attempts_; These represent persistent authentication state that is not properly invalidated. This is CWE-613.

Previous answer did not identify the unhandled exception in variable parsing. Source lines 725-732 show: auto tokens = string_utils::StringTokenizer(command).tokenize(); if (tokens.size() >= 4 && tokens[1] == "set") { ... double value = std::stod(tokens[3]); ... }. The std::stod call has no try/catch, so invalid numeric input will throw std::invalid_argument or std::out_of_range unhandled. This is CWE-754.

Previous answer did not identify integer underflow in memory-pool block split. Source lines 132-136 show: constexpr size_t SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES = 64; size_t remaining = previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES; if (remaining > 32) { fragment.data = base + size + SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES; fragment.size = remaining; }. If size + SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES > previous_size, the subtraction underflows (since size_t is unsigned), producing a large remaining value and writing fragment.data far past the block's actual data. This is CWE-191.

Previous answer did not identify unbounded recursion in the parser. Source lines 381-433 show recursive parse_expression -> parse_term -> parse_factor -> parse_expression with no recursion depth limit. Deeply nested parenthesized expressions would cause stack overflow. This is CWE-674.

Previous answer did not identify the data race in result cache. Source lines 440-452 show: std::unordered_map<std::string, double> result_cache_; mutable std::mutex cache_mutex_; In evaluate_cached(), result_cache_.find(expression) at line 446 is not protected by the mutex, and evaluate() at line 448 is called without holding cache_mutex_. Concurrent calls can cause undefined behavior. This is CWE-362.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Finding 10: 'Time-of-Check-Time-of-Use Race Condition in ConfigLoader', CWE-367, severity Medium, confidence 0.75. Evidence: 'Line 544: if (!std::filesystem::exists(full_path)) return false; checks file existence. Line 545: std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(25)); introduces a delay. Line 546: std::ifstream file(full_path); opens the file. Between the exists() check and the file open, the file could be deleted or replaced with a symlink.'
Items.Notes: Source lines 544-546 exactly match. The TOCTOU race condition with the sleep_for delay is correctly identified.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Finding 11: 'Integer Division by Zero in calculate_route_score', CWE-368, severity Medium, confidence 0.9. Evidence: 'Line 750: size_t token_end = input.find_first_of(" \t"); and Line 751: size_t token_length = (token_end == std::string::npos) ? input.length() : token_end; - if input is empty or contains only whitespace, token_length will be 0. Line 752: return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length); divides by zero when token_length is 0.'
Items.Notes: Source lines 750-752 exactly match. The division by zero vulnerability is correctly identified.
